data.title: Hunting PLC acquires Organic Oil Recovery Technology for $17.5M
European markets dip after a volatile week
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.59% to 8,631. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.64% to 23,035.

France (CAC:IND) -1.11% to 8,104. France imports and exports remained stable in January.

Slovakia’s economy advanced by 1.8% Y/Y in Q4.

Industrial production in Spain shrank 1% Y/Y in January.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.98% lower to 550.6, rounding off a volatile week due to ongoing uncertainty surrounding US trade policy under the Trump administration, which continued to weigh on investor sentiment. Luxury stocks were among the worst performers.

The European Central Bank made the anticipated interest rate cut  but took a more cautious approach to future rate changes while raising its near-term inflation forecasts.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area GDP expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 7 basis points to 2.82%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.67%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: SpaceX's Starship explodes after launch again, debris disrupts Florida flights
data.description:
SpaceX's (SPACE) Starship exploded in space minutes after takeoff on Thursday, marking the second unsuccessful test in a row for the massive rocket, as the explosion scattered debris over parts of the Caribbean.

"During Starship's ascent burn, the vehicle experienced a rapid unscheduled disassembly and contact was lost," the Elon Musk-owned company said.

SpaceX (SPACE) later said an "energetic event" in the rocket's aft portion resulted in the loss of several Raptor engines. "This in turn led to a loss of attitude control and ultimately a loss of communications with Starship."

The Federal Aviation Administration "briefly slowed aircraft outside the area where space vehicle debris was falling or stopped aircraft at their departure location." Normal operations have since resumed.

The explosion led to flight disruptions at Florida's busiest airports, as well as flight diversions around the Turks and Caicos Islands. SpaceX (SPACE) said the debris did not contain toxic materials. 

The FAA directed SpaceX (SPACE) to carry out a "mishap investigation" into the incident. "We will conduct a thorough investigation, in coordination with the FAA, and implement corrective actions," the company said.

Meanwhile, the Super Heavy booster separated from Starship's upper stage and returned to Earth as planned, where it was caught by the mechanical arms of "Mechazilla" - the 400-ft rocket-catching structure at Starbase in Texas. This marked the third successful catch of a Super Heavy booster.

data.title: France imports and exports remained stable in January
data.description:
France's trade balance remains at -€5.6B in January 2025 after three months of improvement. Over the month, exports and imports were stable: €50.7B for exports and €56.3B for imports.The energy balance deteriorated slightly (-€0.1B) after a continuous improvement since June 2024. The balance of manufactured products, driven by exports, records a slight increase which offsets the decrease in the energy balance (+€0.1B).The balance of capital goods continues to improve (+€0.2 B after +€0.4B in December 2024). The balance of intermediate goods decreases (-€0.1B) and that of consumer goods remains at its December 2024 level.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Malaysia's Sagtec Global prices IPO at $4 per share
Aeromexico February Traffic: Passenger numbers and load factor fall
Cryptocurrency may likely remain under pressure over near term - JPM
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has cited that the cryptocurrencies may likely remain under pressure in the near term in a recent research note published on Wednesday. Analysts pointed out that following Trump's announcement that he will add Ripple (XRP-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and Cardano (ADA-USD) to a possible strategic crypto reserve alongside bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD), cryptocurrency markets saw a significant recovery. However, this announcement was quickly followed by skepticism.

That said, central banks around the world have also been apprehensive. While Singapore has rejected cryptocurrencies as being incompatible with its long-term investment strategy, the Swiss National Bank and the central bank of Poland have rejected bitcoin as a reserve asset. "In addition, the European Central Bank's criticism of bitcoin reserves highlights the broader skepticism among policymakers about the adoption of cryptocurrencies as reserve assets," the report added.

"February's close to 20% decline in the bitcoin price was accompanied by heavy spot bitcoin ETFs selling, suggesting that retail investors have played a significant role in the recent correction," the analyst wrote. "February's $3.5bn spot bitcoin ETF outflow was the highest monthly outflow since their launch."

The capital markets also exhibit caution, with concerns about market saturation rising alongside Strategy's (previously MicroStrategy) most recent $2B convertible debt issue. The analyst pointed out that the company's more investor-friendly terms indicate dwindling interest. With Strategy's stock down 40% from its November peak and crypto miners raising large amounts of debt and equity, appetite for new fundraising could decline further, the analysts said.

Overall, the analysts predict that the cryptocurrency markets will continue to be under pressure in the foreseeable future in the absence of any obvious positive catalysts. The report also cited skepticism about the feasibility of establishing a U.S. strategic crypto reserve, particularly due to doubts over congressional approval.

To note, President Trump on Thursday evening established a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and a United States Digital Asset Stockpile through an executive order.

Tickers to track: Grayscale Bitcoin ETF Trust (GBTC); ProShares Bitcoin ETF (BITO); Strategy (MSTR); Coinbase Global (COIN); Bakkt (BKKT); Galaxy Digital (OTCPK:BRPHF); MARA (MARA); and Riot Platforms (RIOT).

data.title: Asia markets in red, mirroring Wall Street losses on trade uncertainty and tariff policy shifts
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets in red on Friday, mirroring global market sentiment as investors grappled with further uncertainty from President Donald Trump’s fluctuating tariff policies ahead of the U.S. jobs report.

Bangladesh’s annual inflation rate slowed for the third consecutive month to 9.32% in February 2025 from 9.94% in the previous month. 

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 2.26% to below 36,900 on Friday, sliding toward its lowest levels since September. The Japanese yen strengthened past 148 per dollar on Friday, reaching a five-month-high.

Japan is set to officially declare the end of long-term deflation, Economy Minister Ryosei Akazawa said on Friday, marking a significant shift in the government's economic stance that could help determine the timing of the Bank of Japan’s next interest rate hike.

Japan’s foreign reserves increased by $12.65 billion to $1.25 trillion in February 2025, hitting the highest level in five months.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.25% struggled for clear direction on Friday but were both set to end the week with gains, and the offshore yuan edged lower to around 7.24 per dollar, extending its losses from the previous session as investors absorbed the latest economic data while remaining wary of escalating global trade tensions.

On Friday, official figures revealed that China’s trade surplus surpassed forecasts, surging USD 170.51 billion for January-February 2025, with exports growing by 2.3% and imports shrinking by 8.4%. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.66% to 24,324 in early trading on Friday, breaking a three-day winning streak amid losses mainly from financials.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.11% to 74,513 in morning trade on Friday, gaining for the third straight session, supported by metals, media, auto, and pharmaceuticals.

Australia (AS51) fell 1.81% closing at 7,948 and reaching its lowest level in six months, driven largely by heavy losses in the banking sector, as Australian banks faced criticism for their inflated valuations, with the sector's price-to-earnings ratio trading at a notable premium compared to global peers. The Australian dollar dipped toward $0.63 on Friday, reversing earlier gains from the week.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended lower over President Donald Trump’s fluctuating tariff policies.

U.S. stock futures rose on Friday as investors looked to the highly anticipated monthly jobs report for further insights into the health of the economy: Dow -0.01%; S&P 500 +0.10%; Nasdaq +0.20%.

On the data front, traders are closely watching Friday's monthly jobs report after ADP data revealed that the US private sector added just 77,000 jobs in February, the smallest increase in seven months.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices weakened below $90,000 on Friday, giving back gains from earlier in the week despite US President Donald Trump signing an executive order to create a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China's trade surplus surpass forecasts, exports grow by 2.3% and imports shrink

  • Australia’s trade balance exceeds expectations, export growth accelerates and imports decline

  • Australia’s economy grows by 0.6% in Q4, surpasses expectations

  • Japan’s services sector grows at fastest pace in six months, driven by strong demand

  • China services growth unexpectedly strengthens; sets 2025 GDP growth target around 5%

data.title: Celanese upsizes and prices $2.6B senior unsecured notes
data.description:
Celanese (NYSE:CE) said that its subsidiary, Celanese US Holdings has priced registered offerings of $2.6B aggregate principal amount of notes, including $700M aggregate principal amount of 6.500% senior notes due 2030, $1.1B aggregate principal amount of 6.750% senior notes due 2033 and €750M (or ~$810M) aggregate principal amount of 5.000% senior notes due 2031.

The offering was upsized from $2B to $2.6B aggregate principal amount of notes.

The notes will be guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by the company and certain of the company’s wholly owned domestic subsidiaries.

The offering is expected to close on or about March 14, 2025.

The net proceeds together with borrowings under the company’s 364-day term loan credit agreement will be used to pay certain outstanding senior notes and other indebtedness.
